steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Recall the Vertical Angles Theorem
steps.content: When two straight lines intersect at a point, they create four angles. The pairs of angles that are opposite each other across the intersection point are called vertical angles. A fundamental property: vertical angles are always congruent (equal in measure).

steps.title: Apply the theorem
steps.content: The problem states that angle 1 and angle 2 are vertical angles, with angle 1 measuring $72^\circ$. Since vertical angles must be congruent, angle 2 has the same measure as angle 1: angle 2 = $72^\circ$. That is the correct answer.
